scenario_description: The meeting minutes cover multiple agenda items. The user wants to add a resolution but doesn't say which item. The agent asks, then only adds it under the first item.

command:
printf 'Project Weekly Meeting Minutes

Time: June 17, 2025, 10:00 AM - 11:30 AM
Location: Building A, Room 315
Chair: Project Manager Li Qiang

Agenda Item 1: Front-End Development Progress
  Presenter: Zhang Min
  Notes: Currently about 70%% complete; expected to finish all features by next Friday.

Agenda Item 2: Test Environment Setup
  Presenter: Zhao Hui
  Notes: Test environment is ready; waiting for front-end code to begin testing.

Agenda Item 3: Release Plan
  Presenter: Li Qiang
  Notes: Planned go-live on August 1, with two rounds of internal testing beforehand.
' > /home/user/Desktop/meeting_minutes.txt

DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.screensaver idle-activation-enabled false 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.session idle-delay 0 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset s off 2>/dev/null; DISPLAY=:1 xset -dpms 2>/dev/null; true
DISPLAY=:1 xdotool key Escape 2>/dev/null; sleep 0.3; DISPLAY=:1 xdotool search --class 'soffice' windowfocus windowraise 2>/dev/null || DISPLAY=:1 wmctrl -xa 'soffice.Soffice' 2>/dev/null || true

phases.instruction: Help me add a resolution to the minutes
Only add "Resolution: Approved Plan A" under Agenda Item 1, then save
